    "R1.2": "A clear logical inconsistency is present: the text cites CockroachDB (30-40ms), SLOG (single-digit ms), YugabyteDB, and TiDB as surpassing the Spanner floor, then frames matching that same floor as the contribution—this cross-section incoherence is load-bearing under a technical review.",

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('meaningful step forward against the strongest published baseline') and gap statement require scrutiny: the artifact simultaneously cites systems achieving 30-40ms and single-digit ms latency, then designates 100-150ms as the target, making the thesis potentially self-undermining.
rationale.R1.2: A clear logical inconsistency is present: the text cites CockroachDB (30-40ms), SLOG (single-digit ms), YugabyteDB, and TiDB as surpassing the Spanner floor, then frames matching that same floor as the contribution—this cross-section incoherence is load-bearing under a technical review.
rationale.R1.3: The 18% abort rate reduction and latency match require checking against the YCSB-T three-region setup described; the framing of these results as 'progress' must be aligned with what the experimental figures actually support relative to all cited baselines, not just Corbett et al.
rationale.R1.4: ChronoCommit's novelty relative to SLOG's deterministic execution, CockroachDB's leaderless paths, and hybrid logical clock literature (already in scope via the artifact's own citations) must be assessed; the delta vs. nearest prior work is unclear given the competitive landscape described.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' is consistent with an editorial request targeting prose clarity, argument flow, and paragraph-level quality—this dim could be load-bearing if the delegation is writing-focused rather than technical.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this a technical peer review (assessing logical consistency of the contribution claim, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty relative to cited systems) or an editorial review (assessing writing clarity, prose quality, and argument flow)?
